actual.name: Midnight Deployment Outage
actual.description: Bad deployment at 00:00; first degradation alerts at 01:30 AM; team chose to investigate rather than roll back; full outage cascade began at 02:47 AM and lasted 3.5 hours

conditions.value: Not taken; team chose to investigate

outcomes.description: Full service outage lasting 3.5 hours, affecting 100% of users

counterfactuals.name: Rollback at 01:45 AM
counterfactuals.description: Team initiates rollback to v4.2.0 at 01:45 AM, 15 minutes after first alert and before the cascade failure threshold at ~02:30 AM

conditions.value: Rollback to v4.2.0 initiated at 01:45 AM
Never reached — rollback completes at 02:10 AM

outcomes.description: ~25 minutes of service degradation during rollback; no full outage; cascade failure threshold never reached

differences.counterfactual: ~25 minutes partial degradation, no full outage

differences.actual: 100% of users fully blocked for 3.5 hours
differences.counterfactual: Partial degradation affecting a subset of requests; no complete service loss

insights: The rollback was technically available at 01:45 AM — a 77-minute window existed between first alert (01:30 AM) and cascade failure (02:47 AM)
The cost of the early rollback (25 min degradation) is an order of magnitude lower than the actual outage cost (3.5 hours)
Cascade failure became inevitable around 02:30 AM; the decision window effectively closed 60 minutes before the outage appeared
lessons: Establish a pre-agreed rollback trigger: if alerts fire within 90 minutes of a deployment and root cause is not identified within 15 minutes, initiate rollback by default
Reduce rollback cost (automation, faster procedures) to lower the threshold for making this decision without heroics
Instrument the cascade failure threshold explicitly so engineers have a measurable tripwire rather than a judgment call under pressure

interventionPoint.description: Initiate rollback to v4.2.0 immediately after first alert confirmation, without waiting for root cause identification
interventionPoint.timing: 01:30–02:00 AM window (77 minutes before cascade failure threshold)
